title: Node Test Runner, Runtime Faults, Compile Rejections, Async Leaks, and CLI Tests · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A mobile library technology team maintaining donated computers. They need versioned bounded collection files, pure migration models, safe Node transfer, accessible exports, SSRF and terminal defenses, runtime fault cases, compile-negative evidence, and installed CLI tests. Planned artifact: Deliver Offline Inclusive Catalog Migration with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Define versioned text or JSON export with headings or structured fields, provenance, no control injection, and bounded size.
Bound bytes, parse into unknown, validate own keys and every member, reject duplicates and future versions, then commit state.
Use own-property checks, explicit field construction, safe records, and no blind assignment from unknown objects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — A project TypeScript team handles case 6564 during an out-of-order Promise fault rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate runtime, compile-negative, async, emitted CLI, and cleanup evidence on changed cases; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then change one compiler, Node runtime, emitted artifact, package, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test emitted command stdout, stderr, status, EOF, signal, working directory, listener and handle cleanup, and unhandled rejection policy.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Async Cli Leak Tests precondition or invariant that the project case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A project TypeScript team handles case 6041 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate runtime, compile-negative, async, emitted CLI, and cleanup evidence on changed cases; unknown external values must not cross the runtime validator; then change one unknown JSON field, nested member, status, URL, or byte budget and re-run validation. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test emitted command stdout, stderr, status, EOF, signal, working directory, listener and handle cleanup, and unhandled rejection policy.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Importing and calling main proves packaged Node CLI behavior.
